protocols.name: CPR (Cardiopulmonary Resuscitation)
protocols.when_to_use: Person is unconscious and not breathing
steps: Call 112 immediately or ask someone else to call
Place person on firm, flat surface
Kneel beside the person's chest
Place heel of one hand on center of chest
Place other hand on top, interlock fingers
Keep arms straight, position shoulders above hands
Push hard and fast - at least 2 inches deep
Push at rate of 100-120 compressions per minute
Continue until help arrives or person starts breathing
warnings: Do not stop compressions unless person shows signs of life
If trained, give 2 rescue breaths after every 30 compressions

protocols.name: Choking - Heimlich Maneuver
protocols.when_to_use: Person cannot breathe, cough, or speak
steps: Stand behind the person
Wrap arms around their waist
Make a fist with one hand, place above navel
Grasp fist with other hand
Give quick, upward thrusts
Repeat until object is expelled
Call 112 if object doesn't come out
warnings: Do not slap the back if person is standing
For pregnant women or obese persons, give chest thrusts instead

protocols.name: Fire Emergency
steps: Alert everyone - shout 'Fire!'
Call 101 (Fire) or 112 (Emergency)
If fire is small and you have extinguisher, use PASS method:
P - Pull the pin
A - Aim at base of fire
S - Squeeze handle
S - Sweep side to side
If fire is large, evacuate immediately
Stay low to avoid smoke
Feel doors before opening (if hot, don't open)
Use stairs, never elevator
Once out, stay out - don't go back
warnings: Never use water on electrical or oil fires
Smoke is more dangerous than flames
